title: Tech Action General Biweekly Meeting Jan 14th
description: Tech Action general virtual biweekly meeting to discuss upcoming events, ways to get plugged in, current tech policy news, and more! See the running agenda for what we're discussing this week.

title: Jan 20th - TA Political Education Committee Meeting
description:
The Tech Action Political Education committee meets the third Tuesday of every month. Our education sessions sits at the center of tech, socialism and/or current events, covering topics like AI in healthcare, Privacy/Surveillance, Data centers, Labor organizing in tech and more.

If you are a DSA member who is interested in planning, hosting or helping with day-of volunteering logistics, do attend one of our committee meetings!

This is an in-person meeting only.

title: Internet for All Working Session
description:
Looking for a way to get involved with the Internet for All campaign? Join us on a campaign working session where we'll put our heads together on research, strategy, building out I4A's field material, and more!

Together we can make universal, municipally owned, broadband internet a reality for New York City!

title: Tech Action General Biweekly Meeting Jan 28th
description: Join us for our bi-weekly meeting!

title: Salon with Molly Crabapple: The Jewish Labor Bund and the Hidden History of Jewish Antizionism
description:
The Jewish Labor Bund and the hidden history of Jewish Anti-Zionism

In a collaboration with the NYC-DSA citywide chapter, including our International Committee and Political Education, your Tech Action working group is excited to bring back returning guest Molly Crabapple for a thoughtful conversation on her upcoming book, "Here Where We Live Is Our Country."

NOTE: The Verso Office has limited capacity, and we are now capped for In-Person attendance. We recommend arriving by 7pm to secure your seat, and for everyone else, we encourage you to join us through virtually through our Zoom link.

"Though the Bund celebrated Eastern European Jews as a people, they irreconcilably opposed the establishment of a Jewish state in Palestine. The diaspora was home, the Bund argued. Jews could never escape their problems by the dispossession of others.

"Instead, Bundists created the doctrine of do'ikayt or 'Hereness.' Jews had the right to live in freedom and dignity wherever it was they stood. They would fight for a better and more beautiful world, even alongside people they had been raised to see as enemies."

"Despite vast differences in the worlds that we inhabited, Bundists seemed to me like kin. Like them, I knew the floor of a police cell, the boredom of a leftist meeting, the electric charge of passing a pamphlet to a stranger, the high of believing, rightly or wrongly, that you are about to change the world."

"In the story of the Bund- across decades and geographies, ages, and faiths- I found the story of our own time, a blueprint for survival, a cautionary tale of death, and a philosophy that might yet save us."

We are excited to mention there'll be a special live guest performance by the two-time Emmy award winning musician Max Fractal. Come join us at the Verso office on February 3rd- Doors will open at 7pm, and the event will start by 7:30pm.

A special thanks to Verso Books in hosting this event, please check them out here and follow their socials (IG and Twitter). Be sure to follow Molly on IG, her substack Ink Vault, and visit her shop.

PREORDER THE BOOK NOW

title: Hacknight
description:
Remember those hackathons when you built "apps" powered by pizza, friends and three hours of sleep? It's time to relive those memories – now with DSA comrades. Come meet fellow socialist developers, brainstorm radical civic tech, and build out open-source tools for Tech Action and allies. We'll also be working on policy hacks and data projects. Pizza provided!

If you're already in the loop, click here to view and add to our list of current projects ;)

Solidarity,

Tech Action Organizing Committee

tech.action@socialists.nyc
Instagram: @NYCDSATechAction
Twitter: @NYCDSATechWG
Bluesky: @NYCDSATech.bsky.social
location: Albert and Vera List Academic Center

title: Tech Action General Biweekly Meeting Feb 11th
description: Join us for our bi-weekly meeting!

title: Kudzu Club Workshop: On Culture and Critical Theory
description:
Do you ever find yourself thinking, AI THIS, AI THAT, AYE... I NEED A BREAK?????

Welcome to the (kudzu?!) club 🎯 you're not alone! AI is everywhere these days: it's in our search engines, our emails, our memes, our screens, but there's this near ubiquitous cultural impact to say absolutely nothing of its social and economic impact. This teach-in/workshop uses the cultural phenomenon of AI as a starting point to discuss its role as a tool and weapon of capitalism in accelerating and intensifying trends of alienation, extraction, and exploitation.

Join us on Thursday, Feb. 12 at the Williamsburgh Library Auditorium (240 Division Ave at Marcy Ave) from 6-8PM to learn more & discuss together 👥🌱

-----

Tech Action is happy to support the launch of the Kudzu Club- an initiative run by friends of the working group, Annie and Helen. Be sure to follow them on IG. From Kudzu Club: We do workshops on culture through the lens of critical + marxist theory, find us at the intersection of the base and superstructure near you!

Of note, this event will be in-person only.

title: Feb 18th - TA Policy & Advocacy Committee Meeting
description:
The Tech Action Policy and Advocacy committee meets on a biweekly cadence, with our next meeting on February 18th 2026. We will use this space to push for comprehensive, proactive legislation on digital technology that protects the public from technological harms.

Our work will include internal Research & Communications, as well as external work in Coalition Building and Direct Action. Join us to learn more and get involved!
